---
title: "CV"
---

## Education

+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|              |                                                                       |
+==============+=======================================================================+
| 2025-present | **University of Minnesota**                                           |
|              |                                                                       |
|              | Ph.D. Student in Ecology, Evolution, & Behavior                       |
+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 2020-2024    | **Emory University**                                                  |
|              |                                                                       |
|              | B.S. Environmental Sciences: Ecology & Conservation; Statistics Minor |
+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

## Research Interests

- Ecology, evolution, biogeography, and applied conservation of amphibians and rare plants

- *Ex situ* conservation and collections-based research at botanical gardens and arboreta

- Field-based, experiential, and community-engaged learning in ecology, evolution, and environmental science

## Research Experience

+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|              |                                                                       |
+==============+=======================================================================+
| 2025-present | **Graduate Research and Teaching Assistant,** University of Minnesota |
+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 2024-2025    | **Research Technician (NSF RaMP),** Atlanta Botanical Garden          |
+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 2021-2024    | **Undergraduate Researcher,** Emory University                        |
+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| May-Aug 2023 | **Hollings Scholar,** Northwest Fisheries Science Center, NOAA        |
+--------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

## Publications

[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=oU-uMaEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=sra)

**Chang, N.J.,** L. Eserman-Campbell, A. Carmichael, A.B. Smith, X. Loy, E.E.D. Coffey, & J. Ojcascastro. Ecological niche modeling reveals niche differentiation and climate vulnerability in two imperiled, sympatric Southern Appalachian carnivorous plants. 2026. *American Journal of Botany* 113:e70194. ​​<https://doi.org/10.1002/ajb2.70194>.

**Chang, N.,** W. Hutchinson, & C. Keogh. 2025*.* Microhabitat use of the Talladega Seal Salamander (*Desmognathus cheaha*) and spatial niche partitioning with a congener in metro-Atlanta headwater streams. *Herpetological Conservation & Biology* 20(2):213-228. \[[PDF](https://www.herpconbio.org/Volume_20/Issue_2/Chang_etal_2025.pdf)\].

### Technical Reports

Hines K., **N.J. Chang,** S. Pierce, & C. Brown. In press. Propagation Protocol: Seed germination and nursery culture protocol for American starvine (Schisandra glabra). Native Plants Journal 26(2):158–162. <https://doi.org/10.3368/npj.26.2.158>.

## Teaching Experience

### Graduate Teaching Assistantships

|             |                                                       |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| Spring 2025 | Flowering Plant Diversity/World Flora (PMB4511). UMN. |

### Undergraduate Teaching Assistantships

+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------+
|              |                                                          |
+==============+==========================================================+
| Spring 2023  | Field Botany (ENVS341). Emory University.                |
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Fall 2022    | Intro to ENVS Field Studies (ENVS131). Emory University. |
+--------------+----------------------------------------------------------+

## Continuing Education & Professional Development

+-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|             |                                                                                              |
+=============+==============================================================================================+
| 2025        | **Applied Plant Conservation Course,** Center for Plant Conservation                         |
+-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 2025        | **IUCN Red List Assessor Certification,** International Union for the Conservation of Nature |
+-------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

## Additional Information

- **Field Skills:** Plant identification; amphibian surveys; incremental soil sampling

- **Software:** R, ArcGIS Pro

- **Laboratory Skills:** DNA isolation, genomic library preparation, qPCR, XRF spectrometry, spectrophotometry, titration, respirometry

- **Languages:** Classical Latin (proficient), German (novice), Middle Egyptian (novice)

- **Interests:** Hiking, camping, horticulture, aquaria, photography
